entry.title: What I Use
entry.content:
Operating System

On my main desktop machine I have Windows 10 dual booted with Arch Linux and just Arch on my Laptop.

Music

I use ncmcpp as my music client together with Mopidy.

Mopidy has an amazing collection of plugins where you can basically combine music from Youtube, Spotify and Soundcloud into one central music player.

Text Editor

I use vim for prettty much anything. In conjuction with plugins like CoC it’s a fully fledged IDE, but runs fast.

Browser

qutebrowser for most of my browsing needs. The browser has vim keybindings and a lot of customisation options.

Document Writing

I use LateX for all of my academic papers. There is a learning curve but if you are still using Microsoft Word, I suggest to give it a try.

Music Production

FL Studio is the program I’m most familiar and basically, enjoy. Sadly, there are no sensible Linux alternatives for music production, yet.

Image Editing

GIMP is perfect for basic to advanced photo editing.

Font

My favorite font has to be Cozette. It looks great on any context, plus it includes glyphs compatible with ranger-icons. Most people will tell you bitmap fonts are only good for ricing but they are definetely wrong. Looking cool is definently a bonus.

Terminal Emulator

Alacritty is great. It works out of the box and has a simple, readable config file.

Window Manager

i3 is the first and last window manager I’ve installed.
